meta.phase-name: Visual composer foundations and builder shell

problem.item: The first staff-safe composer exists, but the product model is still partly implicit. Current page data comes from the Payload page template plus Grime Time block work, while the editor UX still behaves like a drawer-centric CMS tool more than a true block builder.

The Structure tab, insertion flow, and top-bar controls do not yet match the intended Softr-like mental model. There is a real dropdown layering bug, redundant page/draft UI, and no fully formalized block library flow.

Before more UX work lands, the system needs an audit of the real existing blocks and sections already represented in the site and template so the block registry grows from actual product data instead of abstract speculation.
Reusable blocks, dynamic blocks, container slots, and custom HTML support need a stable registry contract and insertion model first, even if their deeper behaviors ship in later phases.

goals.goal: Audit the current page-composer and existing page/template sections into a formal block inventory grounded in real implemented content.
Define the durable visual-composer foundations: page model, block instance model, block registry contract, reusable-block boundary, insertion target model, and UI state shape.
Refactor the builder shell so page-level identity and controls live in the header, not redundantly inside Structure or Publish.
Turn Structure into a true block organizer with ordered rows, quick actions, and Softr-style insertion points instead of one-off “Add reusable section” patterns.
Ship the first categorized block browser with `dynamic`, `static`, and `container` groups and a first-class `Custom HTML` block entry in the taxonomy.
Use one rendering and selection model that stays compatible with the existing live composer and production block renderers.

non-goals.item: Do not finish every inline editing behavior in this phase; that moves to the next phase once the shell and registry are stable.
Do not overbuild a fully generic query builder for dynamic blocks.
Do not add arbitrary styling freedom that breaks brand guardrails.
Do not treat reusable block behavior as solved before the registry, insertion model, and shell are coherent.

product-rules.item: A page is an ordered vertical list of blocks.
Use “Page”, “Block”, “Block library”, “Reusable block”, “Instance”, “Slot”, “Draft”, and “Published version” consistently.
The composer uses a three-surface model: preview canvas, right-side composer rail, and block-library drawer/modal.
The Structure tab exists only to answer: what blocks are on this page, in what order, where can I insert another one, what is selected, and what structural actions can I take.
Page-level information belongs in the header. Structure must not repeat the current page identity.
Softr-style insertion is the rule: plus between blocks, plus at end, block browser opens at exact insertion target, inserted block becomes selected.

block-audit.item: Inventory the currently represented sections/components in the composer and page rendering path.
For each one, classify current name, current purpose, block category (`static`, `dynamic`, `container`, or `custom HTML`), schema needs, media needs, reuse needs, and slot/nesting needs.
Use this audit to seed the first formal block registry instead of inventing a detached system.

registry-contract.item: Create or extend a central block registry responsible for label, category, description, icon, reusable support, slot definitions, default data, schema metadata, and preview renderer references.
The registry must support `dynamic`, `static`, and `container` categories, with `Custom HTML` treated as a first-class block type in the browser and structure list.
Dynamic blocks should stay pre-wired per use case for the MVP rather than exposing a general query builder immediately.

builder-shell.item: The top composer header should own compact page title, slug, working page, create draft, visibility, and route preview controls.
Fix the working-page dropdown layering bug so menus render above the composer shell in every tab.
Remove redundant draft explanation text and remove page-name duplication inside Structure.

structure-model.item: Structure shows only ordered page blocks and their hierarchy where applicable.
Each row should expose block label, type, icon if available, and badges such as reusable, hidden, dynamic, or custom HTML.
Quick actions: add below, duplicate, delete, hide/show, and drag reorder.
Insertion points between blocks and at page end open the categorized block browser.

state-model.item: Persisted state: page document, blocks, content, settings, media, visibility, reusable references.
Ephemeral state: selected block id, active tab, preview mode, insertion target, expanded structure ids, block-library open state, dirty state, current page id, current draft id.
Use the existing page/layout data and render pipeline where possible to minimize drift between preview and live output.

definition-of-done.item: The current composer/page system has a documented block audit grounded in real existing sections.
The block registry contract and UI state model are explicit and reference actual existing Grime Time blocks.
The top bar owns page-level controls cleanly, the dropdown layering bug is fixed, and redundant page/draft messaging is removed.
The Structure tab behaves like a real block organizer rather than a mixed settings panel.
A categorized block browser exists for insertion flow, including a visible `Custom HTML` block entry in the taxonomy.
The new shell remains compatible with the current page-composer rendering path and existing saved page data.

tests-required.item: Integration or component coverage for block insertion targeting, top-bar page controls, and structure ordering behavior.
Browser verification for dropdown layering, Structure insertion points, and block-browser open/insert flow.
`npm.cmd exec -- tsc --noEmit` and focused composer integration tests must pass.

open-questions.item: Should `Custom HTML` live under `static` in the initial browser taxonomy or surface as a distinct advanced group later while remaining a first-class block type in the data model?
Which existing sections should be promoted to reusable-block-capable definitions first after the audit?
How much container depth is worth supporting in the first registry contract before nested editing becomes too confusing?

tasks.task: Audit the current composer/page implementation and inventory all existing sections/components into the first real block map.
Define the block registry, block metadata contract, page/block/reusable-block model boundaries, and editor UI state shape.
Fix the working-page dropdown layering bug and move page-level controls into a compact builder header.
Redesign the Structure tab into a clean block organizer: remove redundant page labels, remove the standalone “Add reusable section” pattern, and add structural quick actions.
Build the categorized block browser and insertion-target flow for `dynamic`, `static`, and `container` blocks, including `Custom HTML` in the taxonomy.
Normalize the currently implemented Grime Time blocks into the registry and browser so insertion uses real block definitions rather than ad hoc templates.
Add verification for the builder foundations, shell, and insertion flow.
